== * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町8-34', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsutsumine-jinja}} [[JA:堤根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11427995}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} '''Tsutsumine Shrine''' (つつみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It was founded as the guardian of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}}, and is a Shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. However, there are two Tsutsumine Shrines in Kadoma, and both claim to be the Shikinaisha. === Tsutsumine Shrine (Miyanochō, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tsutsumine-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill||lt=Gyōki Road|UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill||lt=camphor tree|UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill||lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{DEFAULTSORT:つつみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{DEFAULTSORT:つつみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町8-34', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsutsumine-jinja}} [[JA:堤根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11427995}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} '''Tsutsumine Shrine''' (つつみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It was founded as the guardian of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}}, and is a Shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. However, there are two Tsutsumine Shrines in Kadoma, and both claim to be the Shikinaisha. === Tsutsumine Shrine (Miyanochō, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tsutsumine-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{DEFAULTSORT:つつみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{DEFAULTSORT:つつみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町8-34', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsutsumine-jinja}} [[JA:堤根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11427995}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} '''Tsutsumine Shrine''' (つつみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It was founded as the guardian of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}}, and is a Shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. However, there are two Tsutsumine Shrines in Kadoma, and both claim to be the Shikinaisha. === Tsutsumine Shrine (Miyanochō, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tsutsumine-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{DEFAULTSORT:つつみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It was founded as the guardian of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, and is a Shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349|1=Nagai Naomasa}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777|1=1594}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932|1=1725}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996|1=1738}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275|1=Mishima Shrine (Taito)}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{DEFAULTSORT:つつみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{DEFAULTSORT:つつみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * つつみねじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Tsutsumine-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町8-34', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsutsumine-jinja}} [[JA:堤根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11427995}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} '''Tsutsumine Shrine''' (つつみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was founded as the guardian of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, and is a Shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. However, there are two Tsutsumine Shrines in Kadoma, and both claim to be the Shikinaisha. === Tsutsumine Shrine (Miyanochō, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tsutsumine-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349|1=Nagai Naomasa}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777|1=1594}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932|1=1725}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996|1=1738}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275|1=Mishima Shrine (Taito)}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{DEFAULTSORT:つつみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349|1=Nagai Naomasa}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777|1=1594}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932|1=1725}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996|1=1738}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275|1=Mishima Shrine (Taito)}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * つつみねじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Tsutsumine-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町8-34', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsutsumine-jinja}} [[JA:堤根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11427995}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} '''Tsutsumine Shrine''' (つつみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was founded as the guardian of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, and is a Shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. However, there are two Tsutsumine Shrines in Kadoma, and both claim to be the Shikinaisha. === Tsutsumine Shrine (Miyanochō, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tsutsumine-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349|1=Nagai Naomasa}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777|1=1594}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932|1=1725}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996|1=1738}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275|1=Mishima Shrine (Taito)}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]]

{{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * つつみねじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Tsutsumine-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町8-34', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsutsumine-jinja}} [[JA:堤根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11427995}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} '''Tsutsumine Shrine''' (つつみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was founded as the guardian of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, and is a Shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. However, there are two Tsutsumine Shrines in Kadoma, and both claim to be the Shikinaisha. === Tsutsumine Shrine (Miyanochō, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tsutsumine-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349|1=Nagai Naomasa}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777|1=1594}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932|1=1725}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996|1=1738}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275|1=Mishima Shrine (Taito)}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]]

In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932|1=1725}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996|1=1738}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275|1=Mishima Shrine (Taito)}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]]

{{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * つつみねじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Tsutsumine-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町8-34', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府門真市宮野町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsutsumine-jinja}} [[JA:堤根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11427995}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} '''Tsutsumine Shrine''' (つつみねじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was founded as the guardian of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, and is a Shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. However, there are two Tsutsumine Shrines in Kadoma, and both claim to be the Shikinaisha. === Tsutsumine Shrine (Miyanochō, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tsutsumine-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349|1=Nagai Naomasa}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777|1=1594}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932|1=1725}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996|1=1738}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275|1=Mishima Shrine (Taito)}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]]

However, there are two Tsutsumine Shrines in Kadoma, and both claim to be the Shikinaisha. === Tsutsumine Shrine (Miyanochō, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tsutsumine-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}8-34 Miyanochō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=3rd Saturday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|41|N|135|36|15|E}}}} }} Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō, Kadoma, stands along the former Manda no Tsutsumi on the south bank of the Furukawa, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Its deities are {{ill|lt=Hiko-yai-no-Mikoto|Q11086120|WD=Q11086120|lt=Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It is said the Manda clan, deeply connected to construction of {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}}, enshrined their ancestral deity Hiko-yaimimi-no-Mikoto as guardian of the embankment. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the mid-Heian period, it was listed first among five shrines of Manda District, Kawachi Province, and was Kadoma's only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In front of the torii passed the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyōki Road|1=UNKNOWN}}, linking {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}} and {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, and it drew worship from old times. In the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} period (early Edo), local lord {{ill|lt=Nagai Naomasa|Q6958349|WD=Q6958349|1=Nagai Naomasa}} (Nagai Shinano-no-kami Naomasa) merged worship of Kankō (Michizane), whom he revered, and thereafter it was called "Tenmangū." In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine. Part of Manda no Tsutsumi remains near the shrine buildings, with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} over 500 years old growing on top. Until early Shōwa, much longer sections of the embankment remained, but they were cut away by housing development. Through efforts of the shrine priest, part was preserved and designated on March 29, Shōwa 48 as the Osaka Prefecture historic site "Traditional Manda no Tsutsumi." Tsutsumine-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Tsutsumine-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii Tsutsumine-jinja, Manda-no-Tsutsumi-1.jpg|Traditional {{ill|lt=Manda no Tsutsumi|Q11617931|WD=Q11617931|1=Mamuta Embankment}} === Tsutsumine Shrine (Hiejima, Kadoma) === {{神社 | 名称=Tsutsumine Shrine | 画像=[[File:堤根神社, Tsuneki Shrine, P1000227.jpg|250px]]
Torii | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kadoma|Q496080|WD=Q496080|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}422 Hiejima | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E|region:JP}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭=3rd Sunday of October | 地図={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tsutsumine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|9.9|N|135|35|22.3|E}}}} }} Its deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeōkami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and Ōnamuchi-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}). The founding date is unknown, but in the cadastral register of {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1594|Q6777|WD=Q6777|1=1594}}), it appears as "Sannō-gū" serving as ujigami of Hiejima and Kuwasai villages. The "Sannō" name is thought to derive from enshrining Ōnamuchi-no-Kami. In the register of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1725|Q6932|WD=Q6932|1=1725}}), it appears as "Yatsurugi Daimyōjin," likely from enshrining Susanoo-no-Kami. A document from Genbun 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|WD=Q6996|1=1738}}) says three seats were enshrined by adding Amaterasu. In the Meiji 12 "Kita-Kawachi District Shrine Property Register," the name appears as "Ryōmura Shrine," and the deity is one seat of Amaterasu. The name came from being ujigami of two villages. In Meiji 41, it was renamed "Tsutsumine Shrine" as an alleged descendant of the Shikinaisha Tsutsumine Shrine. There is no material proving Shikinaisha status, and it is thought the claim was made to avoid {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, then actively promoted at the time; nevertheless, in August of that year it was merged into gōsha {{ill|lt=Mishima Shrine|Q11355275|WD=Q11355275|1=Mishima Shrine (Taito)}} (present 1374 Mitsushima, Kadoma). In Shōwa 21, it separated from Mishima Shrine and was restored at the old site. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tsutsumine-jinja}} === Tsutsumine Shrine in Miyanochō === * [https://tutumine.jimdofree.com/ Tsutsumine Shrine] * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03070tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] === Tsutsumine Shrine in Hiejima === * [https://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/funai_jinja/dai3shibu/kadoma-city/03086tsutsuminejinja.html Tsutsumine Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumine Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:門真市の建築物]] [[Category:門真市の歴史]]
